name: Brushchetta with Sauteed Escarole
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Kalamata olives, pitted and quartered
4 tablespoons Extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling on bread
3 cloves Garlic, peeled and finely chopped
3 cloves Garlic, peeled and cut in half
recipeInstructions:
1. Place raisins in a small bowl and cover with warm water for about 20 minutes or until they plump up. Drain.
2. In a small sauté pan, toast the pine nuts by stirring them frequently over medium-low heat. As soon as the pine nuts are light brown, transfer them to a small dish.
3. Remove any wilted or bruised leaves from escarole. Wash well under cold running water. Drain and cut into strips.
4. Place olive oil, garlic, and escarole into a large saucepan. Season with salt to taste. Sauté over medium-low heat until the escarole is tender, about 10 minutes.
5. A few minutes before the escarole is ready, add the raisins, pine nuts, and black olives and toss.
6. Grill or lightly toast the bread. Rub with the cut side of the garlic cloves and drizzle with olive oil.
7. Distribute the escarole over the grilled bread and serve immediately.
